mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces the relative attractiveness of cash/bonds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the surrounding macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20d forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declines are not statistically higher than returns following increases (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a still-elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals resilient consumer demand persisting despite tight monetary policy, which supports a 'soft landing' repricing and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; the effect requires both conditions jointly because strong RSAFS alone under low rates or weak RSAFS under high rates does not carry the same signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 376051 million with FEDFUNDS at 5.26%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS growth x FEDFUNDS level) shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the joint-trigger subsample does not outperform the RSAFS-only trigger subsample.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, continued growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects supply-side momentum that sustains corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is expected to weaken or invert in early-recession regimes when INDPRO growth lags demand deterioration.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trend and 60d forward SPX return is below 0.03 during identified late-cycle expansion windows, or the sign of the relationship flips positive-to-negative within the same regime classification.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth signals near-term economic strength that produces short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in continued consumption capacity, with effects concentrated in the immediate 5-day window following the payrolls print due to rapid information incorporation.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds prior 6-month average by 20%
failure_condition: Triggered 5-day forward returns are not significantly different from unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below trend during a monetary tightening regime signals broad interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that precedes weaker consumption and equity market drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; this mechanism is specific to tightening regimes because in easing regimes HOUST weakness reflects lagged effects already priced by markets.
failure_condition: During tightening-regime triggers, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or mean triggered return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in CPI (CPIAUCSL) month-over-month growth above the recent trend triggers repricing of rate-cut expectations, causing a short-term negative equity market reaction over the following 5 trading days as discount rate expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized growth exceeds trailing 6-month average by more than 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Triggered 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly lower than unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.05), or triggers produce positive returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment (UNRATE) and a federal funds rate still held near cyclical highs (FEDFUNDS) signals that monetary policy remains restrictive even as labor conditions deteriorate, raising recession probability and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; this interaction is regime-specific to late-tightening phases where the Fed has not yet begun easing despite labor weakening.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains within 0.25pp of its 12-month high
regime_hint: late monetary tightening with labor deterioration
failure_condition: Interaction-triggered subsample shows 60d forward returns not significantly different from single-factor (UNRATE-only) triggered subsample, or Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.
